Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber attack orchestrated by the 'Outer Space' campaign targets transportation systems with the aim to disrupt operations and exfiltrate sensitive data. The attackers employ a multi-stage approach, starting with reconnaissance and culminating in significant operational impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Script File, User Account, Target Artifact (Mobile device), Logon Session, Target Artifact (Channel)
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Web Shell, User Account Authentication (Mechanism), Disable or Modify Tools, Mimikatz Tool, Network Service Scanning, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Module Load (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers conduct extensive network scans to identify vulnerabilities in the transportation systems' external interfaces, mapping out potential entry points.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The attackers establish command and control servers camouflaged as legitimate services to support their operation.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2025-5419 (Suggested): Google Chromium V8 contains an out-of-bounds read and write vulnerability affecting multiple web browsers.
stage.description: By compromising a supplier's system, attackers gain access to the transportation network through a vulnerable web browser component.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-1364 (Suggested): Type confusion vulnerability in Google Chromium V8 Engine.
stage.description: Attackers exploit known vulnerabilities in public-facing transportation management systems to execute malicious payloads.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: To maintain access, attackers deploy a web shell on a compromised server, enabling them to execute commands remotely.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Using stolen credentials, attackers elevate their privileges within the network to gain administrative control.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Attackers impair defensive measures by disabling or modifying security tools to avoid detection.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: Attackers extract plaintext passwords and hashes from memory to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Mapping the internal network helps attackers plan lateral movements to critical systems.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: Utilizing remote services like RDP or VPNs, attackers move laterally across the network to reach sensitive areas.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: Attackers systematically gather sensitive data from repositories and databases to enrich their knowledge of the targeted environment.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data collected is transferred to attacker-controlled servers via established command and control channels.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Finally, attackers disrupt operational capabilities of transportation systems to cause maximum impact.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space campaign employs a complex, multi-stage cyber attack targeting transportation systems. Starting with reconnaissance and resource development, they infiltrate networks, maintain persistence, escalate privileges, evade defenses, access credentials, discover valuable resources, move laterally, collect sensitive data, and finally exfiltrate it while impacting operations.
